content.problemStatement:
You are tasked with modeling a class hierarchy to represent various aspects of a school system. The hierarchy consists of four classes: A, B, C, and D. Each class has specific characteristics:
- A (Class A): This is the virtual base class that contains an integer data member a. It has a constructor that takes an integer argument to initialize a. It also has a virtual function display() to display the value of a.
- B (Class B): This class inherits from A and adds an integer data member b. It has a constructor that takes two integer arguments, one for initializing a (inherited from A) and the other for initializing b. It overrides the display() function to display both a and b.
- C (Class C): This class also inherits from A and adds an integer data member c. It has a constructor that takes two integer arguments, one for initializing a (inherited from A) and the other for initializing c. It overrides the display() function to display both a and c.
- D (Class D): This class inherits from both B and C. It has a constructor that takes three integer arguments, one for initializing a (inherited from A), one for initializing b (inherited from B), and one for initializing c (inherited from C). 

### Task
Given a,b,c as input, Implement these classes and create an instance of class D with integer values for a, b, and c. Then, call the display() function of the D object to print the output.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat: First line contain 3 integers a, b and c respectively.
content.outputFormat: Call the display() function of the D object to print the output.
